title: W056 table namespace anchor collision and workspace semantics
description: Outcome: close the namespace/anchor edge cases that make node-associated TreeCalc tables behave like Excel tables anchored in a workbook sheet while still preserving TreeCalc ownership. Gate: OxCalc supports multiple tables in one workspace, multiple tables attached to one or nearby nodes, table-name collisions with node names/host names/defined names under the consumed W074 W051/W056 mapping, table rename and virtual anchor movement, workspace-qualified table references, workspace aliases and first-position ! interactions where admitted, and deterministic canonical/display path behavior with bracket escaping and case/canonicalization. Required interface facts: stable table_id/table_node_id, virtual workbook/sheet/anchor identity, table namespace version, caller table context identity, collision diagnostics, cross-workspace availability version, replay identity, and source-span/token preservation for structured refs. Required scenarios: same-name collision diagnostics or typed shadowing under the current W074 handoff; table path vs bare host-name distinction; structured refs in formulas on nodes outside the table, inside data rows, inside totals rows, and across workspaces; rename/move/delete invalidation; save/reopen stability. Dependencies: consumes closed calc-4vs8.32 for current name/call collision mapping where relevant and calc-8tox for workspace-qualified carrier facts; must not parse OxFml private strings or duplicate precedence rules.
acceptance_criteria: Table namespace/anchor scenarios cover same-workspace, same-node/nearby-node, cross-workspace, alias/first-position !, rename/move/delete, save/reopen, inside-table, outside-table, and totals-row contexts; resolver output preserves stable table_id/table_node_id, virtual workbook/sheet/anchor identity, table namespace version, caller table context identity, collision diagnostics, cross-workspace availability version, replay identity, and source span/token facts; current W074 W051/W056 host-name mapping is consumed without adding an OxCalc precedence mirror or TreeCalc-specific OxFml/OxFunc branch; remaining Excel-observation or retained-replay gaps are recorded as typed blockers, not inferred locally.
close_reason: Closed W056 table namespace/anchor/workspace semantics intake for declared scope. OxCalc now consumes closed resolver/namespace evidence (calc-4vs8.47), virtual-anchor identity (calc-4vs8.45), table dependency/invalidation matrix (calc-4vs8.50), OxXlPlay oracle intake (calc-4vs8.52), W074 host-name mapping intake (calc-4vs8.32), DnaTreeCalc table corpus/bridge evidence (calc-4vs8.51), OxReplay retained comparison evidence (calc-4vs8.53), and workspace-qualified carrier facts (calc-8tox). Runtime diagnostics were updated from W074-gated to W074-mapped adjacency codes/messages so explicit structured table selectors stay distinct from bare host-name/callable lanes without an OxCalc precedence mirror. Non-claim: final full-table promotion remains calc-4vs8.43, with cross-producer namespace/anchor/workspace pairing, typed projection gaps, Excel COM limits, and BLK-REPLAY-003 still final-audit risks. Evidence: cargo fmt --check; cargo test -p oxcalc-core table_catalog_resolver -- --nocapture; cargo test -p oxcalc-core virtual_anchor_identity_contract -- --nocapture; cargo test -p oxcalc-core; br lint calc-4vs8.42 calc-4vs8.43 calc-4vs8.47 calc-4vs8.52 calc-4vs8.32 and later calc-4vs8.51/calc-4vs8.53; br dep cycles; git diff --check; fresh-eyes review returned CLOSE after stale W074/.51 status text was fixed.

title: W056 full intended table support final audit
description:
Outcome: perform the final full-intended table support audit after both the third-pass residuals and the fourth-pass comprehensive table completion spine land, so the project can distinguish the earlier declared table-topic promotion from complete long-term product table support.

Gate: calc-4vs8.39 empty-body support, calc-4vs8.40 full admitted function implementation evidence, calc-4vs8.41 DnaTreeCalc lifecycle bridge acceptance, calc-4vs8.42 namespace/anchor/cross-workspace table semantics, and calc-4vs8.44 through calc-4vs8.56 comprehensive table beads are closed or have explicit user-accepted typed exclusions. Promotion criteria: retained evidence covers table structure, formulas, functions, lifecycle updates, persistence, replay/diff/explain, Excel observation, source span/token preservation, prepared identity, dependency/invalidation facts, dynamic table rebind/INDIRECT behavior, UDF/registry impact, and no-host DnaOneCalc guardrails where relevant. title: W056 table whole-system architecture and ownership map
description:
Outcome: turn the node-associated table design into the controlling W056 execution map before any further table implementation is promoted. This bead owns the whole-system slice: DnaTreeCalc table-as-node product model, OxCalc table custody and virtual Excel-anchor projection, OxFml generic structured-reference parsing/binding, OxFunc opaque ReferenceLike function semantics, OxXlPlay Excel observation, OxReplay retained comparison, and DnaOneCalc no-host guardrails.

Design scope: document the desired end state where a DnaTreeCalc node table is exposed to OxFml like an Excel ListObject anchored at a virtual cell, while TreeCalc identity, table membership, row/column identity, namespace versioning, caller context, and invalidation remain OxCalc-owned. The map must explicitly reject private shims, formula-string parsing in host repos, duplicated structured-reference grammar, TreeCalc branches in OxFml/OxFunc, and eager materialization as closure evidence.

Acceptance: W056 workset text or the active spec states the responsibility split, typed interface flow, update/rebind lifecycle, cache/prepared identity inputs, and cross-repo promotion gates. It links the successor beads in this spine and names counterpart repo work or handoffs needed in OxFml, OxFunc, DnaTreeCalc, OxXlPlay, OxReplay, OxVba, and DnaOneCalc. Fresh-eyes review must confirm the design is architectural, not a checklist or parallel path.
acceptance_criteria: W056 spec/workset records cross-repo responsibility split, virtual Excel-table architecture, typed interfaces, update lifecycle, prepared identity inputs, promotion gates, and fresh-eyes rejection of shims or semantic mirrors.
close_reason: Closed after adding W056 Section 4B.3 whole-system node-table architecture map and OxCalcTree host-contract Section 3.10. Evidence: responsibility split across DnaTreeCalc/OxCalc/OxFml/OxFunc/DnaOneCalc/OxXlPlay/OxReplay/OxVba, typed interface flow, update/rebind lifecycle, prepared/cache identity inputs, hard non-goals, and promotion gates for calc-4vs8.45 through calc-4vs8.56. Fresh-eyes review found the map architectural rather than a checklist and no shim/semantic-mirror path is authorized. Checks: br lint calc-4vs8.44, br dep cycles, git diff --check.

title: W056 node-table virtual Excel anchor and identity contract
description:
Outcome: define and implement the stable identity contract that lets a TreeCalc node table behave like an Excel table anchored at a cell for OxFml-facing structured references.

Required contract: stable table handle, table node id, table name/canonical display name, virtual workbook/sheet identity, virtual anchor cell, virtual table range, header/data/totals region refs, row membership identity, row order identity, column identity, table structure version, table value version, and caller row identity. The virtual anchor must be deterministic and replay-visible but must not imply that TreeCalc table semantics are stored as worksheet cells.

Update scenarios: table create/delete/rename/move, table range resize, row insert/delete/reorder, column insert/delete/reorder/rename, totals toggle, header edit, save/reopen, workspace alias changes, and node path changes. Acceptance requires tests or typed evidence that unchanged logical table identity survives non-semantic moves where intended, while namespace/anchor/range identities invalidate prepared artifacts when the observed structured-reference meaning can change.
acceptance_criteria: Stable table/node/anchor/range/row/column/region/version identities are specified or typed missing; update scenarios state stability and invalidation; replay-visible identity facts exist.
close_reason: Closed after documenting the W056 virtual-anchor identity contract in Section 4B.4 and adding focused executable coverage. Evidence: virtual_anchor_identity_contract_separates_table_namespace_anchor_and_membership_changes proves unchanged save/reopen stability, namespace/path invalidation, workspace alias anchor invalidation, anchor movement, row reorder membership stability, and row insertion membership/order/range changes. Existing table update/lifecycle tests cover the remaining update matrix. Checks: cargo fmt --check; focused identity and table-context mutation tests; cargo test -p oxcalc-core; br lint calc-4vs8.45; br dep cycles; git diff --check.

title: W056 generic structured-reference packet contract for node tables
description:
Outcome: freeze the OxCalc-consumed shape of generic OxFml structured-reference packets for node-associated TreeCalc tables without importing TreeCalc semantics into OxFml.

Packet coverage: explicit table name/path, omitted table name, current-row forms, #Headers, #Data, #Totals, #All, #This Row, single column, column ranges, composite section+column forms, escaped table names, escaped column names, exact source_span_utf8, source_token_text, token kind, stable host/table bind handle, opaque selector payload, selected sections/regions/columns, effective bound table identity, caller-context dependency, replay identity, and typed diagnostics.

Acceptance: OxCalc can lower the packet without parsing structured-reference formula text. Missing or inconsistent facts produce typed blockers/diagnostics instead of best-effort inference. The bead must record the exact OxFml counterpart evidence or handoff needed and must keep W074 name/call precedence separate from explicit structured-reference packet admission.
acceptance_criteria: Generic OxFml structured-reference packets cover all node-table forms and preserve source, handle, selection, caller-context, diagnostics, and replay facts; OxCalc lowers without formula parsing.
close_reason:
Product status: implemented scoped W056 generic structured-reference packet contract for node tables. OxCalc preserves and consumes the public OxFml source_token_kind on TreeCalc table prebinds and StructuredTableReferenceIntake without asking OxFml to parse TreeCalc table paths.

Evidence: cargo fmt --check; focused oxcalc-core tests for prebind source preservation and OxFml bind-record lowering; cargo test -p oxcalc-core; git diff --check; br lint calc-4vs8.46; br dep cycles.

Fresh-eyes review: found a seam-doc overclaim that put source_token_kind on generic host-reference bind output rather than only structured-reference packets; fixed in CORE_ENGINE_OXFML_SEAM.md before closure.

Pre-close review: packet facts remain generic; OxFml owns structured-reference parsing/binding; OxCalc owns TreeCalc table path projection, selector payload, dependency/lifecycle facts, and sparse-reader lowering; W074 name/call precedence remains separate.

Self-audit: scope_complete for calc-4vs8.46; target_complete for packet contract and tests; integration_complete with OxFml fml-ds0.16; open_lanes remain successor W056 beads calc-4vs8.47+ and W074 bare-name/callable precedence.

title: W056 table catalog resolver and namespace versioning
description:
Outcome: make the OxCalc table catalog and resolver output complete for node-associated tables and workspace-qualified table lookup.

Resolver output shape: stable table/reference handle, opaque selector, resolution layer, shape hint, effective table identity, virtual anchor identity, caller_context_dependency, optional caller_context_id, host_namespace_version, table_namespace_version, structure_context_version, workspace availability version, and typed diagnostics. The resolver must distinguish table names, node paths, workspace aliases, root/workspace anchors, omitted-table row context, and unavailable workspace/table states without hardcoding OxFml grammar.

Acceptance: table lookup and namespace mutation scenarios are covered for same-node table, ancestor/path-qualified table, cross-workspace table, table/name collisions, table/function/defined-name adjacency, workspace alias mutation, and table deletion. Name/call precedence that depends on Excel oracle evidence remains gated by OxFml W074; explicit structured-reference packet routing must stay unblocked.
acceptance_criteria: OxCalc resolver emits stable handle, opaque selector, layer, shape hint, effective table/anchor, caller context, namespace versions, availability, and diagnostics for table/path/workspace/collision scenarios.
close_reason: Implemented OxCalc W056 table catalog resolver and namespace versioning. Evidence: cargo fmt --check; cargo test -p oxcalc-core table_catalog_resolver -- --nocapture (8 focused resolver tests); cargo test -p oxcalc-core (238 unit tests + 5 upstream-host scaffolding tests); git diff --check; br lint calc-4vs8.47. Fresh-eyes review found exact stable-id, caller-region identity, and coverage gaps; all were fixed before closure. W074 name/call precedence remains gated; this bead only closes explicit table catalog resolution/versioning.

title: W056 full table ReferenceLike reader surface
description:
Outcome: complete the sparse/reference-reader surface for every admitted structured table selection so functions can consume table references without dense/eager materialization.

Reader scope: whole table, data body, selected data columns, multi-column ranges, headers, totals, #All, current row, omitted-table current row, empty data-body tables, single-row tables, sparse blank cells, defined empty strings, typed error cells, row/column order preservation, and stable reader identity. Required methods remain declared_extent, defined_cardinality, defined_iter, read_at(coord) -> Defined(EvalValue) | Blank, contains(coord), and reader_identity.

Acceptance: SUM, COUNT, COUNTA, COUNTBLANK and the admitted wider range-taking functions receive opaque ReferenceLike carriers through generic OxFml/OxFunc paths. OxFunc must not inspect TreeCalc selectors or table packets. Any function that still forces materialization must be listed with a typed exclusion and successor bead.
acceptance_criteria: Sparse ReferenceLike readers exist or typed exclusions are filed for all admitted table selections; admitted functions consume opaque generic carriers; materializing paths are listed with owners.
close_reason: Implemented and validated the OxCalc W056 full table ReferenceLike reader surface. Evidence: cargo fmt --check; cargo test -p oxcalc-core table_sparse -- --nocapture (8 focused reader/runtime tests); cargo test -p oxcalc-core (241 core unit tests plus 5 upstream-host scaffolding tests); git diff --check; br lint calc-4vs8.48; br dep cycles. Fresh-eyes review found no material issues; residual standalone header/totals risk was removed with dedicated assertions. Wider OxFunc function groups remain typed counterpart/successor scope; no eager materialization or TreeCalc selector leakage.

title: W056 table formula row-context and prepared identity
description:
Outcome: finish the execution model for formulas stored on table columns and totals rows, using one formula definition evaluated under row-specific generic OxFml table context.

Required behavior: per-row caller_table_region, current-row resolution, omitted-table resolution, LET/LAMBDA lexical locals remaining OxFml-internal, stable dispatch skeleton reuse across rows, prepared identity variation by table/caller context where required, totals-row context, out-of-data-row current-row rejection, and no DnaOneCalc host-reference requirement for ordinary single-formula formulas.

Prepared/cache identity must include host_namespace_version, table_namespace_version where relevant, structure_context_version, caller_context_id, registry_snapshot_identity, resolution_rule_version, and function registry/capability profile identity. Acceptance requires focused runtime tests and replay-visible identity facts for row insert/delete/reorder, caller row movement, table rename, and registry mutation.
acceptance_criteria: Per-row and totals formulas execute with row-specific generic OxFml context; LET/LAMBDA remains internal; DnaOneCalc no-host use survives; prepared identity includes all relevant host/table/caller/registry/profile inputs.
close_reason: Implemented and validated OxCalc W056 table formula row-context and prepared identity. Runtime now uses OxFml parse/bind StructuredReferenceBindRecord packets for structured references, then OxCalc supplies sparse ReferenceLike/current-row bindings and OxCalc-owned caller/table facts. Prepared identity facts expose dialect, capability profile, host namespace, table namespace, structure context, caller context, resolution rule, actual OxFunc registry snapshot, capability overlay, prepared formula key, dispatch skeleton, and plan template. Evidence: cargo fmt --check; git diff --check; br lint calc-4vs8.49; br dep cycles; cargo test -p oxcalc-core table_formula -- --nocapture; cargo test -p oxcalc-core table_column_formula_runtime -- --nocapture; cargo test -p oxcalc-core table_totals_formula_runtime -- --nocapture; cargo test -p oxcalc-core structured_table -- --nocapture (49 tests); cargo test -p oxcalc-core (242 core tests plus 5 upstream-host scaffolding tests). Fresh-eyes re-review found no material findings. LET/LAMBDA and DnaOneCalc no-host behavior remain OxFml/DnaOneCalc guardrail scope, not OxCalc host-side semantics.

title: BLK-W056 upstream packet/oracle surfaces for full reference runtime closure missing
description:
W056 table-lowering, node-associated table support, the direct-context boundary cleanup, DnaTreeCalc W005 skeleton/save-reopen/click-through closure, and DnaTreeCalc structural-edit propagation runner activation are no longer blockers for this bead. Full W056 reference-runtime closure remains blocked only on the non-table reference completion spine.

Current exact red categories from the executable W056 gate:
- focused direct-context slices green but broader breadth and retained evidence still missing: walk-up/dotted descent, ancestor/root anchors, escaping/canonicalization/case, meta snapshot breadth, sibling navigation, ordered set selectors, recursive descent, reference literals/arrays, bare host names, structural-edit rebind/propagation, and unresolved/invalid/self-reference diagnostics.
- direct-context typed-pending/exclusion lanes that still need product admission or an explicit final exclusion: workspace alias/first-position ! syntax, dynamic INDIRECT/CTRO, cross-workspace runtime references, node-as-function/lambda-valued host nodes, and TreeCalc capability profile gating.
- retained non-table OxReplay/DnaTreeCalc artifacts are still missing for the whole admitted suite.

Already admitted and no longer blockers here: ChildrenV1 and qualified children packets; structured table packets/table lowering/table evidence; direct OxCalcTreeContext product path for @CHILDREN/.*, walk-up focused non-cell-like host names, ancestor-anchor focused forms, bracket-escaped paths, meta invisibility/accessors, @PREV/@NEXT focused forms, ordered selectors, recursive selector tails, reference-only literal arrays, and structural-edit propagation consequences; resolved ordered selector carriers; traversal resolver/bounds; explicit host path base resolution; public cross-workspace availability/degradation packet shape; workspace provider/alias/first-position ! packet model; workspace-qualified carriers/dependency/prepared-identity facts via calc-8tox; reference literal array carriers with typed mixed-array exclusion via calc-4vs8.31; product-neutral bare host-name bind packets mapped to the defined-name / defined-name-LAMBDA lane through OxFml RuntimeHostNameBinding; and the current W074 W051/W056 handoff consumed by calc-4vs8.32.

Closure condition: calc-4vs8.33 must either clear each red category through direct-context product evidence plus retained replay, or record a deliberate typed exclusion accepted by parent W056. The ignored red/green gate `w056_non_table_reference_resolution_full_scope_red_green_gate` remains the executable closure guard.

title: W056 CTRO intake for FEC reference-text dynamic references
description: Outcome: connect the OxFunc/OxFml FunctionExecutionContext reference-text resolver lane for INDIRECT-style dynamic references to OxCalc CTRO/effective-graph overlay facts. OxCalc remains the owner of TreeCalc reference-text resolution and must return ReferenceLike handles while recording dynamic dependency consequences for the resolved target. Required design: resolution request identity includes reference text, mode, a1_style, caller_context_id, host_namespace_version, structure_context_version, resolution_rule_version, and registry_snapshot_identity where relevant; resolved output correlates to host_ref_handle/formal reference handle, shape hint, resolution layer, typed diagnostics, and opaque selector. CTRO facts must distinguish text-input dependency, namespace/structure dependency, resolved membership/order/value edges, target switch old->new edge removals/additions, unresolved/invalid target reclassification, and CTRO-created cycle/release handling. Acceptance: direct OxCalc tests exercise INDIRECT target switch, unresolved->resolved, resolved->unresolved, structural rename/move/delete, membership/order/value invalidation, and self/cycle introduction; OxFml/OxFunc remain TreeCalc-neutral; retained W056 evidence records the CTRO dynamic-reference delta rather than hiding it in value dereference.
acceptance_criteria: 1. OxCalc's TreeCalc ReferenceTextResolver implementation returns ReferenceLike handles without parsing formula syntax outside OxFml. 2. Resolution request identity records reference text, mode, a1_style, caller_context_id, host_namespace_version, structure_context_version, resolution_rule_version, and registry_snapshot_identity where relevant. 3. CTRO facts distinguish text-input dependency, namespace/structure dependency, resolved membership/order/value edges, target switch old->new edge removals/additions, unresolved/invalid target reclassification, and CTRO-created cycle/release handling. 4. Direct OxCalc tests cover INDIRECT target switch, unresolved->resolved, resolved->unresolved, structural rename/move/delete, membership/order/value invalidation, and self/cycle introduction. 5. OxFml/OxFunc remain TreeCalc-neutral and retained W056 evidence records dynamic-reference topology deltas instead of hiding them in value dereference.

title: W056 complete table dependency and invalidation matrix
description:

Dependency facts: table identity, row membership, row order, row value, column identity, column order, header text, header region, data region, totals region, totals value/formula, caller row context, omitted-table enclosing context, virtual anchor/range, workspace availability, and function registry snapshot dependency when formulas call registered functions.

Update matrix: body value edit, formula edit, row insert/delete/reorder, column insert/delete/reorder/rename, header edit, totals toggle/edit, table rename, table move, table delete, table resize, node rename/move/delete, save/reopen, workspace open/close, alias mutation, and structural rebind. Acceptance requires tests or retained evidence showing which readers/prepared formulas invalidate, which remain stable, and which diagnostics are emitted.
acceptance_criteria: Dependency/invalidation facts cover table identity, rows, columns, regions, caller context, anchor, workspace, registry snapshot, and the full table update matrix with evidence for invalidated and stable artifacts.
close_reason: Implemented complete W056 table dependency/invalidation matrix with replay-visible facts, full update/lifecycle scenario coverage, conditional registry snapshot dependency, host-sensitive workspace availability, docs/spec updates, focused and full oxcalc-core tests, and clean fresh-eyes review.

Required action: for each affected repo, either link existing counterpart beads that genuinely cover the hardening scope or create/file a new counterpart handoff/bead request. Dependencies must prevent downstream repos from implementing around missing upstream contracts. The bead must name the promotion order and the evidence each repo owes.

Seam review rule: reject any route that parses another repo's private strings, mirrors another repo's semantics, or creates a temporary bridge that becomes de facto product surface.
acceptance_criteria: Every affected repo has linked counterpart bead/handoff or explicit non-impact rationale; dependency order is recorded; promotion gates and evidence obligations are concrete; coordinator seam review finds no ownership drift, private bridge, or semantic mirror.
close_reason: Closed W056 cross-repo rollout reconciliation: recorded affected repo lanes, counterpart anchors, promotion order, evidence obligations, DnaTreeCalc parent-bead residue, OxFunc/OxReplay adjacent blockers, DnaOneCalc no-host non-impact, and no private parsing/semantic mirror seam rules. Evidence: cargo fmt --all --check; git diff --check; br lint calc-4vs8.62; br dep cycles; focused rollout inventory test; structured_table suite; cargo test -p oxcalc-core; fresh-eyes review CLOSE with no findings.

title: W056 node-table full-feature completion audit after hardening spine
description:
Outcome: perform the final W056 node-table completion audit after the hardening spine has either closed all gaps or converted them into explicit typed exclusions accepted by the coordinator.

Gate: the architecture revalidation, abstraction consolidation, lifecycle matrix, function/UDF integration, oracle/replay convergence, and cross-repo rollout reconciliation beads are all closed. The audit must reconcile prior table closure claims with the hardening evidence and update any stale docs/comments so the table topic status is readable by future agents.

Final status shape: product status, evidence, still-open typed exclusions, formal/proof status, cross-repo evidence pointers, and parent-W056 implication. If remaining work is non-table W056 scope, say so explicitly. If any table item remains unresolved, leave the table topic open with exact blockers rather than using broad completion language.

Promotion rule: full intended node-table support cannot rely on dense materialization, fixture-only readers, private formula parsing, mirrored structured-reference grammar, TreeCalc-specific OxFml/OxFunc branches, Excel-internal inference, or DnaOneCalc regression.
acceptance_criteria: All hardening dependencies are closed or accepted exclusions; final status updates docs/beads with scope/evidence/gaps/formal status; full intended node-table support is either reaffirmed with evidence or left open with exact blockers; parent W056 remaining scope is explicit.
close_reason: Closed W056 node-table final completion audit after hardening spine: table scope reaffirmed for declared W056 node-associated table slice, residuals typed as non-table parent W056 work, DnaTreeCalc parent reconciliation, OxFunc adjacent registry migration, OxReplay value-wire cleanup, and future VBA/XLL extension. Evidence: cargo fmt --all --check; git diff --check; br lint calc-4vs8.63; br dep cycles; focused final audit test; structured_table suite; cargo test -p oxcalc-core; fresh-eyes review CLOSE with no findings.

OxCalc exposes a normal green inventory test plus an ignored red/green full-closure gate that currently fails on remaining non-table blockers. DnaTreeCalc validator and active runner checks are recorded. Parent W056 and calc-4vs8.5 are not falsely closed.

Evidence: cargo fmt --all --check passed; cargo test -p oxcalc-core passed; focused matrix test passed; ignored full-scope red/green gate fails intentionally with the remaining red categories; DnaTreeCalc corpus validator and active-runner sweep passed; fresh-eyes review returned CLOSE with no P0/P1/P2 findings.

title: W056 boundary cleanup: direct OxCalc tree context replaces DnaTreeCalc semantic bridge
description:
Outcome: make the OxCalc/DnaTreeCalc ownership boundary executable. OxCalc owns canonical workspaces, nodes, formula text, structural edits, reference/table resolution, dependency graph, recalculation state, and invalidation. DnaTreeCalc owns UI/product state only and uses exported OxCalc context APIs directly, with no semantic bridge DTOs, prepared carrier construction, or local reference/path resolver.

## Success Criteria
- OxCalc exposes a persistent direct tree context path and the one-shot facade/request/result API is deleted from live source/docs.
- DnaTreeCalc product and active corpus paths no longer depend on OxCalcTreeBridge, LiveOxCalcTreeBridge, TreeRecalcRequest, PreparedFormulaCatalog, or PreparedFormulaReferenceCarrier.
- Bridge-based W056 evidence is reissued through direct OxCalc context or explicitly marked migration-only.
- OxCalc context owns node table custody, workspace snapshot import/export, edit invalidation, and direct recalc/view surfaces.
- No OxFml or OxFunc TreeCalc-specific semantic branch is introduced.
- Boundary guards, repo-local tests, git diff checks, br lint, and br dependency-cycle checks pass before closure.
close_reason: Closed W056 boundary cleanup parent: OxCalc direct OxCalcTreeContext is the only live tree host API shape; one-shot facade/request/result API is deleted; context owns node/table state, snapshot import/export, edit invalidation, recalc and view surfaces; DnaTreeCalc product/corpus bridge deletion and active corpus migration are closed, including direct-context table structured-reference parent dtc-z0i.5. Evidence: cargo fmt --check; cargo test -q -p oxcalc-core; pwsh scripts/check-w056-boundary-cleanup.ps1; git diff --check; br lint calc-4vs8.65; br dep cycles; DnaTreeCalc dtc-z0i.13.2/.13.3/.13.4 and dtc-z0i.5 are closed. Non-claims: W056 remains open for non-table retained evidence/node-as-function and DnaTreeCalc W005 save-reopen/click-through.

title: W056 hard-delete one-shot facade and forbidden bridge seams
description:
Outcome: delete the non-ideal one-shot OxCalcTree facade/request/document compatibility surface and make OxCalc's direct OxCalcTreeContext the only tree host API. Add a guard that fails if wrong-shape bridge/facade/prepared-carrier symbols remain in live source/docs outside bead history.

Scope: remove OxCalcTreeDocument, OxCalcTreeRecalcRequest, OxCalcTreeRecalcResult, OxCalcTreeRuntimeFacade, OxCalcTreeRuntimeError as public/live symbols; refactor OxCalcTreeContext::recalculate to drive LocalTreeCalcEngine directly through private context helpers; convert facade tests to direct-context or lower-level tests; update OxCalc docs touched by the deleted API so only direct context is described.

Acceptance criteria: cargo fmt --check, cargo test -p oxcalc-core, git diff --check, br lint calc-4vs8.65.1, br dep cycles pass; rg guard for forbidden facade/bridge/prepared-carrier terms is clean over OxCalc live src/docs except .beads history; fresh-eyes review finds no retained compatibility wrapper.
notes: Hard deletion pass completed for the OxCalc one-shot tree facade shape. Public/live symbols OxCalcTreeDocument, OxCalcTreeRecalcRequest, OxCalcTreeRecalcResult, OxCalcTreeRuntimeFacade, and OxCalcTreeRuntimeError were removed from consumer.rs; OxCalcTreeContext now drives LocalTreeCalcEngine directly through private context-owned inputs and returns OxCalcTreeCalculationOutcome. OxCalcTreeEnvironment was replaced by OxCalcTreeContextOptions. Facade-oriented tests were converted to direct-context or lower-level engine tests. OxCalc docs and table evidence strings were swept for the forbidden bridge/facade/prepared-carrier identifiers, and scripts/check-w056-boundary-cleanup.ps1 now enforces the guard over src and docs. Fresh-eyes review checked the direct context path, docs contract section, and forbidden-symbol guard; remaining broader DnaTreeCalc bridge deletion and table-custody context APIs stay on successor beads. Evidence: cargo fmt --check; cargo test -p oxcalc-core; pwsh scripts/check-w056-boundary-cleanup.ps1; git diff --check; br lint calc-4vs8.65.1; br dep cycles.
close_reason: Closed hard-deletion bead for OxCalc live source/docs. Direct OxCalcTreeContext is now the only tree host API shape in OxCalc source; the one-shot facade/request/document public surface and forbidden bridge/prepared-carrier names are absent from src/docs under the W056 guard. Evidence passed: cargo fmt --check; cargo test -p oxcalc-core; git diff --check; pwsh scripts/check-w056-boundary-cleanup.ps1; br lint calc-4vs8.65.1; br dep cycles.

title: W056 table custody through OxCalcTreeContext
description:
Outcome: move node-associated table lifecycle, identity/versioning, structured-reference lowering, row/column/header/totals dependency facts, and dynamic table rebind classification behind OxCalc context APIs.

Acceptance criteria: DnaTreeCalc edits table state through OxCalc and reads typed table views/results; DnaTreeCalc product/corpus code no longer calls a semantic table rebind classifier directly.
notes: Implemented context-native table custody in OxCalc. OxCalcTreeContext now stores node-associated TreeCalcTableNodeSnapshot values, derives canonical/display paths and namespace/structure versions from context state, and exposes set_node_table, clear_node_table, table_view, workspace_table_views, table_context_packet, resolve_table_reference, lower_table_reference, lower_table_bind_record, and classify_dynamic_table_rebind. Product callers no longer need to call standalone semantic table projection/catalog/lowering/rebind helpers for the covered table workflow. Added direct-context tests treecalc_context_owns_node_table_lifecycle_and_views and treecalc_context_routes_table_catalog_lowering_and_dynamic_rebind. Updated the consumer contract and W056 status to describe direct-context table custody. Evidence: cargo fmt --check; cargo test -p oxcalc-core treecalc_context_; cargo test -p oxcalc-core; git diff --check; pwsh scripts/check-w056-boundary-cleanup.ps1; br lint calc-4vs8.65.2; br dep cycles.
close_reason: Closed table-custody context bead. OxCalcTreeContext now owns node table snapshots and exposes context-native table view, context packet, catalog resolution, dependency lowering, bind-record lowering, and dynamic rebind classification APIs. Focused context tests and full oxcalc-core checks passed; forbidden boundary-symbol guard remains clean.

title: W056 OxCalc-owned raw TreeCalc formula binding expansion
description:
Outcome: route ordinary raw node formula text through OxCalc/OxFml host binding so DnaTreeCalc does not prepare DirectNode/RelativePath/dynamic/literal/cross-workspace carriers for product formulas.

Acceptance criteria: focused OxCalc tests cover raw bare-name, dotted descent, walk-up, selector, dynamic, cross-workspace, reference literal, and node-as-function families through public OxCalc APIs, or record typed exclusions where upstream evidence is still missing.
notes: Implemented direct OxCalcTreeContext raw binding expansion: lexical walk-up and dotted host-name resolution through OxFml host-name bind packets with source token/span and replay identity; context-owned qualified children and ordered selector resolution; explicit prebound TreeCalc selector spans are excluded from host-name probing. Refactored product-style raw children, qualified children, ordered selector, and recursive selector tests to use OxCalcTreeContext; retained lower-level prepared-carrier tests for internal source-preservation, dependency, and sparse-reader contracts. Fresh-eyes review found source-span, walk-up, case-sensitivity, and brace-overreport issues; all addressed. Evidence: cargo fmt --check; cargo test -p oxcalc-core; git diff --check; br lint calc-4vs8.65.3; br dep cycles.
close_reason: Direct-context raw TreeCalc formula binding expansion implemented and tested. Product-style non-table raw children, qualified children, walk-up/dotted host names, ordered selectors, and recursive selector tests now use OxCalcTreeContext; unresolved dynamic INDIRECT, cross-workspace, reference-literal, and callable host-name lanes are typed pending/exclusion surfaces. Fresh-eyes findings remediated. Evidence: cargo fmt --check; cargo test -p oxcalc-core; git diff --check; br lint calc-4vs8.65.3; br dep cycles.

title: W056 persistent OxCalcTreeContext direct workspace API
description:
Outcome: implement the first persistent OxCalcTreeContext surface for direct host use: create workspace, add nodes with formula_text, recalculate, and read node/workspace views.

Acceptance criteria: OxCalc test creates a context, workspace, A = =3, B = =A+1, recalculates, and observes A=3 and B=4 without DnaTreeCalc carriers. Existing OxCalcTreeRuntimeFacade remains compatible.
notes: Implementation approach: add an additive OxCalcTreeContext API in oxcalc-core consumer surface for the first direct workspace path; keep existing facade compatible; add a red/green test for A:=3, B:=A+1 without DnaTreeCalc carriers. This intentionally starts with the minimal direct context slice and leaves broad raw reference-family migration to calc-4vs8.65.3.
close_reason: Implemented and reviewed the narrow persistent OxCalcTreeContext direct workspace API: create_workspace/add_node/recalculate/node_view support A = =3 and B = =A+1 without DnaTreeCalc prepared carriers. OxFml parse/bind, not runtime execution or private token scanning, supplies unresolved host-name candidates; LET, direct LAMBDA, and LET-held callable LAMBDA lexical names remain OxFml-internal; failed edits do not consume stable node ids. Evidence: cargo fmt --check, cargo test -p oxcalc-core treecalc_context_ -- --nocapture, cargo test -p oxcalc-core, git diff --check, br lint calc-4vs8.65.4, br dep cycles all passed. Fresh-eyes re-review found no blockers for this narrow scope. Follow-up for full host-name bind-result/source-span identity is recorded on calc-4vs8.65.3.

description: Outcome: add an OxCalc-owned workspace snapshot export/import surface on OxCalcTreeContext so DnaTreeCalc save/reopen can preserve OxCalc-issued workspace, node, formula, and table identities without rebuilding private structures. Acceptance: focused round-trip test proves workspace id, stable node ids, formula text, table id/context identity, and published values survive export/import/recalc; cargo fmt --check, cargo test -p oxcalc-core, git diff --check, boundary guard, br lint, br dep cycles pass.

Round-trip test proves workspace id, node ids, formula text, table id/context identity, table namespace version, seeded values, verified-clean recalc, and post-import node allocation. Docs name context-owned snapshot import/export as the save/reopen path. cargo fmt --check, cargo test -p oxcalc-core, git diff --check, W056 boundary guard, br lint, and br dep cycles pass.

acceptance_criteria: OxCalcTreeContext mutation APIs conservatively clear seeded published values so formula, structural, and table edits do not reuse stale dependent values. Focused regression edits A after an initial A/B recalc and proves B republishes as 5. cargo fmt --check, cargo test -p oxcalc-core, git diff --check, W056 boundary guard, br lint, and br dep cycles pass.
close_reason: Implemented conservative seeded-value invalidation for direct context mutations and focused regression proves dependent values republish after formula edit. Evidence: cargo fmt --check; cargo test -p oxcalc-core (264 passed, 1 ignored, upstream_host_scaffolding 5 passed); git diff --check; W056 boundary guard; br lint; br dep cycles. Fresh-eyes review saw no blocking findings in the diff.

title: W056 serializable OxCalc tree workspace snapshot for DnaTreeCalc save-reopen
description:
Outcome: make OxCalcTreeWorkspaceSnapshot serializable so DnaTreeCalc .dnatree save/reopen can persist the OxCalc-owned context snapshot and reopen through import_workspace_snapshot without rebuilding private structural/table state.

## Acceptance Criteria
- OxCalcTreeWorkspaceSnapshot and nested structural/table snapshot types derive serde Serialize/Deserialize where required.
- Round-trip test serializes an exported workspace snapshot to JSON, deserializes it, imports it into OxCalcTreeContext, recalculates, and proves workspace id, node ids/formula text/table identity/value publication survive.
- No one-shot facade/request/result API is restored.
- cargo fmt --check, cargo test -p oxcalc-core, W056 boundary guard, git diff --check, br lint, and br dep cycles pass.
close_reason: Implemented serializable OxCalc tree workspace snapshots for DnaTreeCalc save/reopen. OxCalcTreeWorkspaceSnapshot and required nested structural/table snapshot types now derive Serialize/Deserialize; the export/import test serializes to JSON, deserializes, imports into a fresh OxCalcTreeContext, verifies workspace id, stable node ids/formula text, table id/context identity, seeded value for B, verified-clean recalc, and post-import node allocation. No facade/request/result API restored. Evidence: cargo fmt --check; cargo test -q -p oxcalc-core treecalc_context_export_import_preserves_identity_and_recalc_state -- --nocapture; cargo test -q -p oxcalc-core; pwsh scripts/check-w056-boundary-cleanup.ps1; git diff --check; br lint calc-4vs8.66; br dep cycles.

DnaTreeCalc producer: dtc-z0i.8. Steps: BO1 carry opaque callable node value + display policy; BO2 dereference wiring to supply the stored callable back to OxFml (replace typed_exclusion:node_as_function_w074_pending in consumer.rs ~3227); BO3 build dependency edges from the captured-ref facts OxFml reports on the callable result (depends on fml-ds0.20 surfacing them); BO4 active_node_functions_corpus evidence. PRINCIPLE: OxCalc never parses/introspects formulas; OxFml detects callability, resolves free/bound vars via callbacks, returns a portable callable pointer.

The blocker is not ChildrenV1 execution; it is the missing contract that maps raw qualified base text to a stable TreeNodeId without freezing TreeCalc name/call precedence or mirroring host semantics. Current evidence supports only free-standing owner/caller-based @CHILDREN / .*; a safe successor API should accept a resolved base or resolve explicit path syntax against the OxCalc-owned structural snapshot.
close_reason: Implemented caller-supplied resolved-base prebind contract for qualified ChildrenV1 raw formula syntax. prebind_treecalc_formula_text remains strict and rejects qualified selectors without exact source-span-keyed resolved-base packets; prebind_treecalc_formula_text_with_resolved_bases admits base.@CHILDREN and base.* by full source span, base span, selector span, stable base TreeNodeId, resolution layer, and resolution identity, preserving qualified source token text/span and using the existing ChildrenV1 reference-preserving OxFml/OxFunc path. Remaining W056 blockers: calc-4vs8.4 table row/order/exact regions and calc-4vs8.5 final name/call, structured packet, cross-workspace, and selector dependency surfaces.

Semantic-equivalence statement:
For structural dependency changes, the existing structural rebind gate remains unchanged. For the bounded dynamic dependency CTRO lane, treating dynamic activation/release/reclassification as repairable overlay deltas is semantically equivalent to rebuilding the successor effective graph for the exercised TreeCalc profiles: the target-switch/downstream run publishes the same owner and downstream values as the rebuilt effective graph while preserving atomic publication of value plus overlay consequences. Unsupported dynamic targets, structural cycles, CTRO-created cycles, and grid/spill cases do not publish through an unsafe repair path; they reject, remain exact-blocked, or route to W048/W049 as specified.

title: W050 design/refactor OxCalc OxFml formula authority and call model
description: Design/refactor workset: re-specify and repair the OxCalc/OxFml formula authority boundary and call model. OxFml owns formula parsing, text-literal understanding, programmatic formula construction, binding/name/reference semantics, function/operator evaluation, array/spill values, coercion, volatile behavior, and returned value surfaces. OxCalc owns structural graph state, dependency descriptors, invalidation, CTRO overlays, cycle policy, candidate/publication state, replay evidence, and evaluator-fact plumbing. The workset must decide and document the first-call protocol: what formula artifact/source/handle and structural/name/reference context OxCalc passes, whether OxFml resolves names through supplied context or host callbacks, how unresolved identifiers are surfaced, and what single-node result structure OxCalc consumes.
close_reason: W050 parent workset closed for declared production formula-authority scope. The OxCalc/OxFml seam now uses OxFml runtime prepared-package/template-hole/formal-reference/formal-input surfaces; OxCalc-local compatibility projection is deleted; TreeCalc production invocation no longer uses synthetic A1 cell_values/defined-name reference fixtures; bridge metadata, subscription/topic envelopes, correctness-floor selectors, derivation trace, value-cache/scheduling evidence, rich/capability reservation, and cross-repo receiving decisions are incorporated. Fixture-only Minimal* and legacy FixtureFormulaAst surfaces are retained as test/quarantine support, not production formula-authority debt. Successor work is explicitly routed: W051 Excel-scope sparse readers, W054 bounded memory, W049 formalization, W052 spreadsheet-recognizable sensitivity, and a non-workset future ideas note for beyond-Excel rich/virtual data. Validation: cargo test -p oxcalc-core -- --nocapture passed (132 unit tests, 5 upstream-host tests, doctests); git diff --check passed; scripts/check-worksets.ps1 passed before closure; stale active W050 status search clean. Three-axis: scope_completeness=scope_complete; target_completeness=target_complete; integration_completeness=integrated; open_lanes=none inside W050 production scope.
